author: y4rr
content: Implementation landed in commit c43abd37c (bundled into the #1112 commit by a concurrent agent's 'git commit -a', which swept my unstaged ferrotorch-train edits; the CHANGELOG entry under #1108 in that commit documents this fix fully). All evidence-floor gates pass: cargo test -p ferrotorch-train --lib (113 passed), cargo test -p ferrotorch-train --tests (51 passed including 2 conformance fixture tests + the new discriminating test_checkpoint_sequential_real_checkpoint_grad_fn), cargo clippy -p ferrotorch-train --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo build --workspace (clean). The new discriminating test wraps a CountingScale module with shared AtomicUsize, asserts grad_fn.name() == 'CheckpointBackward' (structural — would fail on the old straight-through forward because Mul would surface at the top of the chain), then runs backward and asserts the counter strictly increased (behavioural — the sabotage-catching signal that recomputation actually fired). Public-API change documented in surface inventory: modules: &[M] -> Vec<Arc<dyn Module<T>>> to satisfy the core checkpoint's 'static + Send + Sync closure bound.
